questions.question: The `bash builtins` -u option read lines from file descriptor fd instead of the standard input.
questions.explanation: Correct. `bash builtins` -u option read lines from file descriptor fd instead of the standard input.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -a option the first character of delim terminates the input line, rather than newline.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `bash builtins` -a option the words are assigned to sequential indices of the array variable aname , starting at 0.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -N option read returns after reading exactly nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out.
questions.explanation: Correct. `bash builtins` -N option read returns after reading exactly nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -t option cause read to time out and return failure if it does not read a complete line of input (or a specified number of characters) within timeout seconds.
questions.explanation: Correct. `bash builtins` -t option cause read to time out and return failure if it does not read a complete line of input (or a specified number of characters) within timeout seconds.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -u option evaluate callback each time quantum lines are read.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -s option if input is coming from a terminal, characters are not echoed.
questions.explanation: Correct. `bash builtins` -s option if input is coming from a terminal, characters are not echoed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -n option read returns after reading exactly nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. `bash builtins` -n option read returns after reading nchars characters rather than waiting for a complete line of input, unless it encounters EOF or read times out, but honors a delimiter if it reads fewer than nchars characters before the delimiter.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -e option if the standard input is coming from a terminal, read uses Readline (see Command Line Editing ) to obtain the line.
questions.explanation: Correct. `bash builtins` -e option if the standard input is coming from a terminal, read uses Readline (see Command Line Editing ) to obtain the line.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -e option if Readline is being used to read the line, read places text into the editing buffer before editing begins.

questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
